Now we have to do abide by something called the four day law. So we set up our campaign, we set up our ad sets, we set up our ads. And now we're just waiting to see if it works. Now in the very beginning, it can be super tempting even for myself not to mess with it. Right. You see something doesn't look like it's going to work and you want to turn it off because you don't want to lose money. Or you see something that's working really good and you want to up the budget because you think it's going to make you a lot of money. But according to the four day law, you cannot touch any of your ad sets for four days. So even if one's just tanking, it's losing you tons of money. You cannot turn it off for four days. And even if one's making you a ton of money, you can't raise the budget for four days. Now the reason why is because something called initial conditions. Now in the beginning ads are just kind of figuring themselves out that Facebook algorithm, which is super complex, more complex than we can ever imagine, is trying to figure out which direction to take your ads. That's who to show your ads to, who's most likely to buy. And you don't know if it's working or not till after four days. An ad that's absolutely just tanking, losing you tons of money might kind of get in the groove by the fourth day and just start rocking and making you tons of money. And an ad that looks profitable from day one might just tank on day four. So we have to wait for four days to figure out which ones are truly working and which ones aren't. Because the data from a day or two days is worthless. It doesn't mean anything. So do not touch your ads for the first four days. Whenever you create a new ad, this goes forever. ## [1:09:38 - 1:12:42]

Once you've had 200 conversions, what we're going to do to take your advertising to the next level is create look-alike audiences. All right. Now, we have all these ad sets before and they're profitable, great. And our goal is to get as many profitable ad sets as possible. And that's what look-alike audiences are going to allow us to do. So here's what a look-alike audience is. So in the past, we had our conversions, people who went to our website and took the desired action. And we have that small group of maybe 200. What a look-alike audience is going to do is it's going to take Facebook's algorithm and it's going to find people just like the people who took the desired action on your page. So say you had 200 conversions, Facebook's going to find a million people exactly like the people who converted on your website. And there's different levels of look-alike audiences. There's people who are 1% look-alike, a 2% look-alike, a 5% look-alike. And so on and so on. Now, the best ones obviously going to be 1% because they're within 1% similarity to the people who already bought something or took the desired action on your website. And what this allows us to do is take our ads from guessing the targeting, guessing, oh, we think it's someone who likes Tony Robbins.
